patches.find: ps-tldr">The dome's custom coordinate system was fitted to the Sydney–Perth railway distance, then fails by 78% on Sydney–Buenos Aires. Two methods within the dome's own model disagree by 460 km on the same city pair — and errors grow toward the disc edge, exactly matching the distortion of projecting a sphere onto a flat surface.
patches.replace: ps-tldr">The simplest geometric test: the dome predicts Antarctica's ~70°S coast would take ~112,000 km to circumnavigate; satellite-tracked ships and racing routes record ~13,700 km — a factor-of-eight discrepancy that refraction cannot explain, because ships move through water, not light. The dome's coordinate system was fitted to the Sydney–Perth railway, then fails by 78% on Sydney–Buenos Aires; two methods within the dome's own model disagree by 460 km on the same city pair, with errors growing toward the disc edge.
patches.rationale: ISS-977 (partial): TLDR for Section 7.1 did not mention the Antarctic rim-circumnavigation argument at all, despite it being the opening and strongest argument in the section. Adding it to the TLDR gives the argument the prominence it deserves and makes the TLDR representative of the section's strongest content.
